data.narrative:
Loft Orbital is a Series C-stage space and aerospace company that has raised $326M to date. Valuation analysis relies on a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector private companies (Sierra Space at $8.0B, Varda Space Industries at $1.5B, and Skyryse at $1.1B), alongside reference to five public Space & Aerospace comparables trading at a median 25.8x price-to-sales.

Two triangulation methods prove applicable. The stage-progression method, using total capital raised as a proxy and applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $554M. The peer-median approach across the three same-stage private comparables yields $1.5B, reflecting the broad dispersion in the peer set ($1.1B to $8.0B). The public-comp anchoring method cannot be applied due to absence of revenue data for the private target.

These two methods triangulate to a range of $471M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $1.0B. The wide variance between stage-progression and peer-median reflects uncertainty around Loft Orbital's competitive positioning relative to peers and the heterogeneity of valuations within early-stage aerospace. Resolution of this range depends on clarity around revenue scale, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to Sierra Space and Varda.
